from sklearn.cluster import KMeans
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np

def task_func(df1, df2, column1="feature1", column2="feature2"):
    # Combine datasets and concatenate the columns
    combined_df = pd.concat([df1[ [column1, column2] ], df2[ [column1, column2] ]])

    # Standardize the features to improve KMeans performance
    scaler = StandardScaler()
    standardized_features = pd.DataFrame(scaler.fit_transform(combined_df[ [column1, column2] ]), columns=[column1, column2])

    # Perform KMeans clustering with the specified parameters
    kmeans = KMeans(n_clusters=2, n_init=10, random_state=42)
    kmeans.fit(standardized_features)

    # Prepare data for scatterplot
    labels = kmeans.labels_
    plot_data = combined_df.merge(pd.DataFrame(labels, columns=["cluster"]), on="id")

    # Create scatterplot and return the figure's Axes object
    plt.figure(figsize=(10, 6))
    plot_data[ [column1, column2, "cluster"] ].plot(kind='scatter', c=plot_data["cluster"], s=50)
    plt.xlabel(column1)
    plt.ylabel(column2)
    ax = plt.gca()

    return tuple((labels, ax))
